description: Bugfix cycle 3 for Milestone 6 — WebSocket & Real-Time. Fixes 1 critical defect: DEF-002 (auth_permission duplicate key IntegrityError in Docker PostgreSQL tests due to keepdb=True preventing serialized_rollback from working).

userStories.title: Fix: DEF-002 — Remove keepdb=True from conftest.py so serialized_rollback works
userStories.description: Fix defect DEF-002 from QA report qa-m6-websocket.md. The conftest.py's django_db_setup uses keepdb=True which skips Django's DB serialization step, making serialized_rollback ineffective and causing auth_permission duplicate key IntegrityError on 12 consumer tests.
acceptanceCriteria: All 32 WebSocket consumer tests pass when run via: docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml run --rm python-tests pytest
Zero test ERRORs related to IntegrityError on auth_permission or django_content_type tables
All other existing tests (non-WebSocket) continue to pass
userStories.notes:
Defect: DEF-002. File: services/gateway/conftest.py. Expected: All 32 consumer tests pass. Actual: 12 ERROR with `IntegrityError: duplicate key value violates unique constraint auth_permission_pkey`.

Root cause: The `django_db_setup` fixture in conftest.py uses `keepdb=True`. With keepdb, `setup_databases()` skips test DB creation AND the serialization step that stores the initial DB state. When `TransactionTestCase._fixture_setup` runs with `serialized_rollback=True`, it calls `deserialize_db_from_string()` which tries to INSERT rows that already exist in the persistent DB.

Fix approach — do ONE of the following (Option A preferred):

Option A (simplest): Remove the `django_db_setup` fixture override entirely from services/gateway/conftest.py. Keep only the `django_db_serialized_rollback` autouse fixture. Let pytest-django's default `django_db_setup` handle DB creation with proper serialization. The Docker postgres testuser has superuser permissions and can CREATE DATABASE.

Option B (if Option A fails due to TEST.NAME conflict): Also update `services/gateway/gateway/settings/test.py` to remove the `TEST` dict from DATABASES, or change `TEST.NAME` to a different name like `test_ziqreq_test`. Currently `TEST.NAME = _db_name` which is `ziqreq_test` — same as the production DB name. pytest-django may need a different name to create the test DB.

Option C (fallback): If removing django_db_setup causes issues, replace it with a version that does NOT use keepdb:
```python
@pytest.fixture(scope='session')
def django_db_setup(django_test_environment, django_db_blocker):
    from django.test.utils import setup_databases, teardown_databases
    with django_db_blocker.unblock():
        db_cfg = setup_databases(verbosity=0, interactive=False, keepdb=False)
    yield
    with django_db_blocker.unblock():
        teardown_databases(db_cfg, verbosity=0)
```

IMPORTANT: After making changes, rebuild the test container: `docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml build python-tests` then run: `docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml run --rm python-tests pytest`

Files: services/gateway/conftest.py (modify), services/gateway/gateway/settings/test.py (possibly modify TEST.NAME)
